case_title: JAMES J FRANICEVICH et al VS. ROBERT HOWARD PETERSON

MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-29-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT ROBERT PETERSON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD AND SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. FOR THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT INTENDED BENEFICIARIES AS THEY ARE NOT THE SUBJECT OF A SPECIFIC BEQUEST BY DEFENDANT ATTORNEY'S NOW DECEASED CLIENT. RATHER PLAINTIFFS ARE POTENTIAL BENEFICIARIES, AND DEFENDANT OWED NO DUTY TO PLAINTIFFS AS POTENTIAL BENEFICIARIES OF HIS CLIENT'S ESTATE. THIS IS BECAUSE "A TESTATOR'S LAWYER [DOES NOT] OWE A DUTY OF CARE TO A NONCLIENT WHO ALLEGES [HE] WAS A POTENTIAL BENEFICIARY OF THE TESTATOR'S ESTATE IN THE ABSENCE OF AN EXECUTED WILL OR TRUST INSTRUMENT EXPRESSLY REFLECTING THE TESTATOR'S INTENT." (CHANG V. LEDERMAN (2009) 172 CAL.APP.4TH 67, 86.) THE CLAIMS FOR LEGAL MALPRACTICE AND CONTRACT FAIL AS A MATTER OF LAW. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION "TORTIOUS INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE," SOUNDS IN NEGLIGENCE, AND PLAINTIFFS CANNOT PREVAIL BECAUSE DEFENDANT OWED THEM NO DUTY, AS SET FORTH ABOVE. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, ENTITLED "INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE," THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION REQUIRES KNOWING INTERFERENCE AND INTENTIONAL CONDUCT THAT WAS WRONGFUL BY SOME MEASURE OTHER THAN THE INTERFERENCE, WHICH IS NOT ALLEGED. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFFS APPEAR IN THEIR MOVING PAPERS TO BE SEEKING TO ALLEGE INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH EXPECTATION OF INHERITANCE. SEE BECKWITH V. DAHL (2012) 205 CAL.APP.4TH 1039, 1057. PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THIS DEFENDANT AND MATERIAL FACTS TO SUPPORT THAT CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2013 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jason E. Fellner, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, Tel. 415.788.1900, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT ROBERT PETERSON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained, without leave to amend, as to the first, second, and third and Sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action. For the first and second causes of action, Plaintiffs are not intended beneficiaries as they are not the subject of a specific bequest by Defendant attorney's now deceased client. Rather Plaintiffs are potential beneficiaries, and Defendant owed no duty to Plaintiffs as potential beneficiaries of his client's estate. This is because "a testator's lawyer [does not] owe a duty of care to a nonclient who alleges [he] was a potential beneficiary of the testator's estate in the absence of an executed will or trust instrument expressly reflecting the testator's intent." (Chang v. Lederman (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 67, 86.) The claims for legal malpractice and contract fail as a matter of law. The third cause of action "tortious interference with prospective economic advantage," sounds in negligence, and Plaintiffs cannot prevail because Defendant owed them no duty, as set forth above. As to the fourth cause of action, entitled "intentional interference with prospective economic advantage," the demurrer is sustained. This cause of action requires knowing interference and intentional conduct that was wrongful by some measure other than the interference, which is not alleged. However, Plaintiffs appear in their moving papers to be seeking to allege intentional interference with expectation of inheritance. See Beckwith v. Dahl (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 1039, 1057. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to plead, if they can do so in good faith, a cause of action against this Defendant and material facts to support that cause of action. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported
